message:
test(fcp-store): pin ObjectId collision detection (mt6ac)

Add crates/fcp-store/tests/object_id_collision.rs pinning the
MemoryObjectStore::put collision-detection contract. Six tests cover:

- forged collision with different body → AlreadyExists carrying the
  conflicting object_id; original body unchanged.
- forged collision with different header → AlreadyExists; header
  unchanged.
- identical re-put (true dedup case) → AlreadyExists with first-writer-
  wins state.
- AlreadyExists Display contains both the "object already exists"
  marker (error.rs:13 format) and the conflicting object_id; Debug
  identifies the variant.
- two distinct object_ids in the same zone coexist (collision is keyed
  on object_id alone).
- a rejected collision MUST NOT advance storage_used — defense-in-depth
  against an attacker burning quota on already-taken ids.

These properties are NORMATIVE per object_store.rs:474 (the
contains_key check) and error.rs:13 (the Display format), and the
contract is what the runtime relies on to reject the attacker-chosen-id
class called out in flywheel_connectors-4g0qr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -38,6 +38,21 @@
       "value": 65536,
       "canonical_hex": "1a00010000",
       "description": "65536 requires 4-byte additional info"
+    },
+    {
+      "value": 4294967295,
+      "canonical_hex": "1affffffff",
+      "description": "Max 4-byte value"
+    },
+    {
+      "value": 4294967296,
+      "canonical_hex": "1b0000000100000000",
+      "description": "First value requiring 8-byte additional info"
+    },
+    {
+      "value": 18446744073709551615,
+      "canonical_hex": "1bffffffffffffffff",
+      "description": "u64::MAX uses 8-byte additional info"
     }
   ],
 
@@ -153,18 +168,21 @@
         "name": "length_first",
         "keys": ["bb", "aaa", "a"],
         "sorted_keys": ["a", "bb", "aaa"],
+        "canonical_hex": "a3616102626262006361616101",
         "description": "Shorter keys come first regardless of lexicographic order"
       },
       {
         "name": "lexicographic_same_length",
         "keys": ["z", "a", "m"],
         "sorted_keys": ["a", "m", "z"],
+        "canonical_hex": "a3616101616d02617a00",
         "description": "Same-length keys sorted lexicographically"
       },
       {
         "name": "mixed",
         "keys": ["zz", "aa", "b", "aaa"],
         "sorted_keys": ["b", "aa", "zz", "aaa"],
+        "canonical_hex": "a461620262616101627a7a006361616103",
         "description": "Length first, then lexicographic"
       }
     ]
